Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the pressure P and the specific volume of the atmosphere are related according to the equation where ‘P’ is N/m2 and is in m3/kg, depth of the atmosphere necessary to produce a pressure of 1 bar at the earth’s surface ________ m. (Round of to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 1333
Solution

1333 (1331 - 1335)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A mass of 2 kg of air is compressed in a quasi-static process from 0.2 MPa to 0.8 MPa for which Pv = constant. Initial density of air is 2 kg/m3, work done by the piston is ______kJ. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 277.26
Solution

277.26 (276.0 - 278.0)

 
 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In a cyclic process, heat transfers are +10 kJ, –25 kJ, –2 kJ and +40 kJ, net work for this cyclic process is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

An ideal gas cycle consists of three reversible processes, starting with constant volume pressure rise, then isentropic expansion to 7 times the initial volume and constant pressure compression up to the initial volume, the efficiency of the cycle will be ______ %. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 35.43
Solution

35.43 (34.21 - 36.59)

 
 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The internal energy of air is given, by u = u0 + 0.7t, where u is in kJ/kg, u0 is any arbitrary value of u at 0°C in kJ/kg and t is temperature in °C, where p is in kPa and is in m3/kg. An evacuated bottle is fitted with a valve through which air from the atmosphere at 101.325 kPa and 25°C is allowed to flow slowly to fill the bottle. If no heat is transferred to or from the air in the bottle, temperature in the bottle when pressure inside bottle reaches 101.325 kPa, is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
 
 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The scales are so choosen that a reversible cycle plots clockwise as circle on the T-s plane as shown in figure. The maximum and minimum values of temperatures are 1000 K and 400 K and the entropy 2.8 and 1.2 kJ/kgK, respectively. The efficiency of the cycle is

 
 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A rigid closed tank of volume 2 m3 contains 3 kg of wet steam at a pressure of 200 kPa. The tank is heated until the steam becomes dry saturated, and pressure inside tank becomes 304 bar. Heat transfer to the tank will be _____ kJ. [Correct upto two decimal places]

 
NAT
Correct answer: 1512.76
Solution

1512.76 (1510.00 - 1515.00)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Tank A has a volume 0.2 m3 and contains steam at 200°C, 20% liquid and 80% vapour by mass,
and tank B is evacuated, as shown in figure. The valve is then opened, and the tanks eventually
come to same pressure, which is found to be 4 bar. During this process, heat is transferred such
that the steam remains at 4 bar, then then volume of tank B is _______ m3. [Correct upto two
decimal places]

 
 
NAT
Correct answer: 23.26
Solution

23.26 (23.10 - 23.40)

 
 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The figure below shows a gas separated from the vacuum by a membrane. The membrane ruptures and the gas fills the entire volume. Considering gas as the system, which among following statements regarding the work done by the gas during this process is(are) TRUE. Neglect any work associated with the rupturing of the membrane.

 
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(c, d)
The process mentioned in the question is the case of free expansion. Since there is no restraining or opposing force or pressure as expansion occurs against vacuum. So work done is zero in case of free expansion process.

Q10 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A gas expands from an initial state where the pressure is 300 kPa and the volume is 0.04 m3 to a final pressure of 150 kPa. The relationship between the pressure and volume of the gas is = constant, workdone for the process is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A closed cylinder of 0.4 m diameter is fitted with a frictionless piston. The piston is retained in position by a catch in the cylinder wall and the volume on one side of the piston contains air at a pressure of 800 kN/m2. The volume on the other side of the piston is evacuated. A helical spring is mounted coaxially with the cylinder in this evacuated space to give a force of 150 N on the piston in this position. The catch is released and the piston travels along the cylinder until it comes to rest after a stroke of 1.5 m. The piston is then held in its position of maximum travel by
a ratchet mechanism. The spring force increases linearly with the piston displacement to a final value of 6 kN. The work done by the compressed air on the piston is

 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following cannot be treated as a pure substance?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
A pure substance is a form of matter that has a uniform composition throughout.

Q13 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A mass of 0.1 kg of an ideal gas has a pressure of 200 kPa, a temperature of 70°C, and a volume of 0.08 m3. The gas undergoes an irreversible adiabatic process to a final pressure of 200 kPa and final volume of 0.2 m3, during which the work done on the gas is 30 kJ, CV of the gas will be

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(c, d)
Heat is not that which inevitably causes a temperature rise, when heat is transferred to an iceand-
water mixture, the temperature does not rise until all the ice has melted.
Process in which no heat crosses the boundary of the system is called adiabatic process.

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

An insulated rigid tank is divided into two equal parts by a partition. Initially, one part contains 5 kg of an ideal gas at 500 kPa and 57°C and the other part is evacuated. The partition is now removed, and the gas expands into entire tank, then final temperature in the tank will

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q16 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A piston-cylinder device, whose piston is resting on set of stops, initially contains 1 kg of air at 200 kPa and 27°C. The mass of piston is such that a pressure of 400 kPa is required to move the piston. Heat is transferred to the air until its volume doubles. Workdone by the air will be ____ kJ. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 172.2
Solution

172.2 (170 - 174)

Not attempted

Two vessels, A and B, both containing oxygen are connected by a valve which is opened to allow the contents to mix and achieve an equilibrium temperature of 27°C. Before mixing the following information is known about the gases in the two vessels. (Assume y = 1.5)

 
 
Which of the following options is/are TRUE?
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
